Calculator.disclaimerText: This calculator processes the standard order of penetration and reduction. However, slight discrepancies from actual in-game values may occur due to complex overlapping mechanics.

Metadata.description: The comprehensive information site for League of Legends: Wild Rift (WildRiftHub), featuring the latest tier lists, patch notes, and champion matchups.

Guide.title: Comprehensive Guide & Basics
Guide.subtitle: From basic rules to advanced macro theory, this is the ultimate guide to winning in Wild Rift.
Guide.beginnerIntroDesc: Wild Rift is a 5v5 team battle MOBA. Each player controls a unique 'Champion', working together to destroy enemy towers and ultimately the enemy 'Nexus' to win the game.
Guide.beginnerMapDesc: The map consists of 3 main lanes and the Jungle in between. Typically, the 5 players divide into the following roles:
Guide.beginnerLaneSoloDesc: Holds the solo lane. Durable tanks or strong duelists (fighters) are usually played here.
Guide.beginnerLaneJungleDesc: Instead of laning, they farm neutral monsters in the forest and ambush (gank) lanes unexpectedly.
Guide.beginnerLaneMidDesc: Holds the short central lane. Mages with high magic damage or burst assassins shine here.
Guide.beginnerLaneDuoDesc: Long-range physical attackers. Weak early, but scale into massive damage dealers in the late game.
Guide.beginnerLaneSupportDesc: Pairs with the Duo (ADC). Their job is to provide vision (wards), heal, and disrupt enemies to help the team.
Guide.beginnerFlowLaningDesc: Start by killing minions in your lane to gain XP and gold for items. Avoid unnecessary deaths and build your strength.
Guide.beginnerFlowMidDesc: Group with your team to destroy towers or slay powerful neutral monsters like Dragons and Rift Herald for team-wide buffs.
Guide.beginnerFlowLateDesc: All 5 players group up for massive 5v5 fights. Slaying the ultimate monster, Baron Nashor, grants a powerful buff used to end the game.
Guide.intermediateDesc: Wild Rift is not just a direct port. Optimized for mobile, its core macro strategies differ significantly from PC.
Guide.intermediatePassiveGoldDesc: In Wild Rift, you receive about 40% of a minion's gold just by standing near it when it dies (PC gives 0). Because of this, 'Freezing' a lane to starve your opponent is much less effective. Instead, 'Push & Roam' is the dominant meta.
Guide.intermediateMapSizeDesc: The map is significantly smaller, meaning rotations between lanes happen twice as fast as on PC. A 1v1 skirmish can turn into a 4v4 teamfight in seconds. Checking the minimap and rotating quickly directly impacts your win rate.
Guide.intermediateItemsDesc: Powerful active effects like Stasis (Zhonya's) or Protobelt are available to everyone cheaply as boot enchantments. This allows ADCs to become invulnerable or tanks to dash, making champions more versatile.
Guide.advancedDesc: Advanced macro and wave management theories practiced by Challenger players, specialized for Wild Rift.
Guide.advancedPriorityTitle: The Absolute Power of Push & Roam (Priority)
Guide.advancedPriorityDesc: Due to passive gold and a smaller map, the strongest tactic is quickly clearing your minions (Pushing) and impacting other lanes (Roaming). Pushing forces the enemy to stay under their tower to farm, granting you 'Priority'—the freedom to move first. This brief window lets you help your jungler or secure vision for Dragon.
Guide.advancedCrashRecallDesc: A common beginner mistake is recalling at the wrong time. Always 'Crash' your wave (push it fully into the enemy tower) before recalling. While you are at base, the enemy tower kills your minions, forcing the enemy to lose gold and XP.
Guide.advancedThreeWaveDesc: Top players are always calculating 1 minute into the future (about 2-3 waves). Before an objective spawns, they create a 'Slow Push' by only last-hitting. This builds a massive wave that crashes into the enemy tower, forcing the enemy to deal with it while your team takes the Dragon with a numbers advantage.
Guide.settingsDesc: Settings for iOS and Android to prevent lag and ensure a smooth experience.
Guide.iosFps: - Frame Rate: Highly recommend '60 FPS'. 120 FPS is smooth but can cause rapid overheating and screen dimming (thermal throttling).
Guide.iosGraphics: - Graphics Quality: 'Medium' or 'High'. Lowering effects slightly drastically saves battery and reduces heat.
Guide.iosFocus: - Focus Mode: Enable iOS 'Gaming Focus Mode' to prevent notification banners from interrupting your touches.
Guide.androidFps: - Frame Rate: '60 FPS'. Unless you have a top-tier gaming phone, locking to 60 FPS provides the most stable performance.
Guide.androidBoost: - System Optimization: Turn on your phone's built-in game booster (e.g., Galaxy Game Booster) to free up memory.
Guide.androidGraphics: - Graphics: If you feel lag during teamfights, lower the resolution and effects to 'Low' or 'Medium'. Performance always beats graphics.
Guide.inGameDesc: Changes in the settings menu that will drastically improve your gameplay and mechanics.
Guide.inGameCameraDesc: Allows you to drag the 'eye icon' to offset your camera, giving you a wider view of where you are going or where the enemies are. Essential for landing skillshots.
Guide.inGameTargetDesc: When tapping the attack button, it automatically targets the enemy with the lowest numerical HP. Makes securing kills much smoother.
Guide.inGameChatDesc: Prevents toxicity from random teammates and allows you to focus 100% on your gameplay. Highly recommended to preserve your sanity.
Guide.inGameDashDesc: When quick-tapping a dash skill, you will dash in the direction your movement joystick is pointing, rather than towards an enemy. Mandatory for escaping safely.
Guide.recomDesc: These champions have simple kits and are excellent for learning the fundamental mechanics of the game.

Modes.aramDesc: All Random All Mid (ARAM) tier list, strongest champions, and exclusive item guide.
Modes.aramRulesDesc: In ARAM, healing is reduced, and returning to base does not heal you. Fast scaling and poke damage are highly valued.
